title: Top 10 Herbal Tinctures for Pain Relief from Frozen Shoulder

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is good for frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the affected shoulder, promoting relaxation of the muscles and tendons.
Polyphenols are good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility.
Tannins are good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the shoulder, making it easier to move and recover from the condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, is a natural ingredient used to make tinctures that may help with frozen shoulder. Frozen shoulder is a condition where the shoulder joint becomes stiff and painful, making it hard to move the arm. Turmeric tinctures contain a compound called curcumin, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory agent. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the shoulder joint. Curcumin also has anti-oxidant properties, which help protect the joint from damage caused by free radicals. When taken as a tincture, curcumin can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the shoulder joint, making it a potential natural remedy for frozen shoulder. Turmeric tinctures are easy to use and can be taken orally, making them a convenient option for people looking for a natural way to manage their frozen shoulder symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Arctium lappa herbal tincture for frozen shoulder relief, you will need dried Arctium lappa roots, a clean glass jar with a lid, a bottle of high-proof vodka or other solvent, and a cheesecloth or a coffee filter. Start by filling the glass jar with 1 part of dried Arctium lappa roots and 2 parts of vodka, making sure the roots are completely covered. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for about 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called maceration, which helps to extract the medicinal properties of the roots into the vodka. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the roots. The resulting tincture can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 6 months and taken sublingually (under the tongue) as needed, usually 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal tinctures for frozen shoulder

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a popular herb used to treat various health issues, including frozen shoulder. A tincture made from ginger is good for frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties. When you have a frozen shoulder, the connective tissue in your shoulder joint becomes inflamed and stiff, causing pain and limited mobility. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce inflammation and relieve pain by blocking the production of certain chemical messengers that promote inflammation. Additionally, ginger has a warming effect on the body, which can help increase blood flow to the affected area and promote healing. This can lead to reduced pain, improved range of motion, and faster recovery from frozen shoulder.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Arctium lappa herbal tincture that may help with frozen shoulder, you will need dried Arctium lappa roots, a clean glass jar with a lid, a piece of muslin cloth or a coffee filter, a glass measuring cup, and a bottle to store the tincture. First, place 1 part of dried Arctium lappa roots into the glass jar. Next, fill the jar with 2 parts of a high-proof spirit, such as vodka or rum, making sure the roots are completely covered. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process allows the spirit to extract the medicinal properties from the roots. After the extraction period, use the muslin cloth or coffee filter to strain the liquid into another glass container. Discard the solids and transfer the tincture to the storage bottle. The resulting Arctium lappa tincture can be taken by mixing a few drops with water or another liquid, ideally 3-5 times a day, to help alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture, also known as licorice root tincture, is a herbal remedy that may help alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder. Frozen shoulder is a condition where the joint capsule around the shoulder becomes inflamed and stiff, making it difficult to move the arm. The tincture is made from the root of the licorice plant, which contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. These compounds, including glycyrrhizin, may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the shoulder joint, making it easier to move the arm and perform daily activities. Additionally, the tincture may help reduce pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for a range of motion and improving overall comfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root tincture may also help reduce scarring and adhesion in the joint capsule, which can contribute to the development of frozen shoulder.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Arctium lappa herbal tinctures that help with frozen shoulder, first gather the necessary ingredients: fresh or dried Arctium lappa roots, a carrier oil like olive or coconut oil, and a solvent like 80% ethanol or vodka. Start by preparing the roots by cleaning and drying them, if using fresh roots. Then, chop the roots into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar. Next, pour the carrier oil over the roots, making sure they are completely covered, and seal the jar. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day, to allow the roots to infuse their properties into the oil. After the infusion process, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. Now, to make the tincture, combine the infused oil with the solvent in a 1:1 ratio in another clean glass jar, such as 1 part of the infused oil to 1 part of ethanol or vodka. Seal the jar and let it sit for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day, allowing the solvent to extract the roots' properties. Finally, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, and discard the solids. The resulting tincture can be used as directed to help alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for frozen shoulder

Polyphenols are good for frozen shoulder because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the shoulder joint.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum tincture, also known as cayenne pepper tincture, is a natural remedy that may help relieve the pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. The main active compound in Capsicum annuum, capsaicin, works by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. When applied topically, capsaicin can penetrate deep into the skin and muscles, providing quick relief from pain and inflammation. Additionally, Capsicum annuum tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and promote blood flow to the affected area, further alleviating pain and stiffness. Some people also find that Capsicum annuum tincture helps to improve mobility and range of motion in the affected shoulder, making it easier to move and perform daily activities. By reducing pain and inflammation, Capsicum annuum tincture may help to promote healing and recovery from frozen shoulder.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Arctium lappa herbal tincture that can help with frozen shoulder, you'll need fresh or dried Burdock root, a clean glass jar with a lid, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a dark glass bottle for storing the tincture. First, prepare the Burdock root by washing it thoroughly and cutting it into small pieces. If using fresh root, dry it in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for 2-3 hours to preserve it. Fill the glass jar with the prepared root, leaving about an inch at the top. Pour the solvent over the root, making sure that the root is completely covered. Secure the lid and let it steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. Store the Arctium lappa tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as directed - typically 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day - to help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana tincture is a natural remedy that may help with frozen shoulder. Frozen shoulder is a condition where the shoulder joint becomes stiff and painful, making it hard to move the arm. Arnica montana is a plant that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. When taken as a tincture, it can be absorbed into the bloodstream and reach the affected joint, where it may help to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The tincture can also help to reduce scar tissue and adhesions that can form in the joint, which can contribute to the stiffness and pain of frozen shoulder. Additionally, Arnica montana has been shown to have a positive effect on the production of collagen, a protein that helps to rebuild and repair damaged tissue. This may help to improve the range of motion and reduce the pain associated with frozen shoulder. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Arnica montana tincture may be a useful natural remedy for managing the symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Arctium lappa herbal tincture to help with frozen shoulder, first you need to gather the dried roots of Arctium lappa plant, also known as burdock root. You can buy these roots from a health food store or online. Next, measure one part of the dried roots to ten parts of a neutral-tasting liquid like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. The ratio is one part herb to ten parts liquid. Fill the jar with the liquid, making sure the roots are completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots release their active compounds into the liquid. After two weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the roots. You now have your Arctium lappa herbal tincture. To use it, mix a few drops of the tincture with water and drink it two to three times a day. It's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Arctium lappa herbal tinctures for frozen shoulder relief, you will need dried Arctium lappa roots, high-proof vodka or another spirit, and a clean glass jar with a lid. First, fill a clean glass jar about one-quarter full with dried Arctium lappa roots. Then, pour vodka or another spirit over the roots until the jar is almost full, leaving about one inch of space at the top. The general rule is to use a 1:5 ratio of herb to spirit, but you can adjust this to your liking. Next, close the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the herbs release their properties into the spirit. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. You can now use the Arctium lappa tincture as directed on the label, typically by taking a few droppers full under your tongue several times a day to help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arctium lappa tincture is a herbal remedy that has been used for centuries to help with frozen shoulder. This condition occurs when the shoulder joint becomes stiff and painful, making it hard to move the arm. The tincture is made from the root of the plant, which contains a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and pain.  When Arctium lappa tincture is taken orally, it is absorbed into the bloodstream and helps to reduce the inflammation in the shoulder joint. This is thought to be due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to calm down the immune system's response, which can cause pain and stiffness in the joint. In addition to reducing inflammation, Arctium lappa tincture may also help to improve range of motion and reduce pain. Some people find that taking the tincture regularly helps to ease the stiffness and pain associated with frozen shoulder, allowing them to move their arm more freely.  Arctium lappa tincture is often used in conjunction with other treatments, such as physical therapy and exercise, to help manage frozen shoulder. It may also be taken in combination with other herbs that have anti-inflammatory properties, such as turmeric and ginger.  The recommended dosage of Arctium lappa tincture varies depending on the individual and the severity of the condition. Some people may find that taking a small dose of 20-30 drops per day is sufficient, while others may need to take a larger dose of 40-60 drops per day. It's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before taking any herbal remedy, especially if you have a pre-existing medical condition or are taking medication.

First, fill the glass jar with 1 part dried Burdock root and 2 parts solvent, making sure the root is fully covered. Then, seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the solvent extract the herbs' medicinal properties. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the clean glass dropper bottle, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 20-30 drops, 3-4 times a day, as needed to help relieve frozen shoulder symptoms.

intro_desc: Frozen shoulder, also known as adhesive capsulitis, is a condition where the shoulder joint becomes stiff and painful, making everyday activities like dressing, showering, and even sleeping difficult. Fixing this issue is essential as it can lead to depression, anxiety, and a significant decrease in quality of life. Herbal tinctures, a liquid extract of plants, can be a natural and effective way to alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder.  Two herbs commonly used in herbal tinctures for frozen shoulder are turmeric (Curcuma longa) and ginger (Zingiber officinale). Turmeric contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the shoulder joint. Ginger, on the other hand, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help relax the muscles and improve blood flow to the affected area. By using a combination of these herbs in a tincture, you can promote healing, reduce pain, and improve mobility in the shoulder joint.  Herbal tinctures can be taken orally, typically mixed with water or tea, and can be used in conjunction with other treatments, such as physical therapy or massage, to achieve the best results. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment, especially if you're already taking medications or have underlying health conditions. With the help of herbal tinctures, you can regain control over your daily activities, reduce pain and discomfort, and improve your overall well-being.
supplementary_best: For frozen shoulder, the best combination of herbal tinctures includes Boswellia serrata, Curcuma longa, and Arnica montana. Boswellia serrata tincture reduces inflammation and pain by preventing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals. Curcuma longa tincture, also known as turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the affected shoulder joint. Arnica montana tincture soothes and calms the muscles and joints, reducing stiffness and pain. This combination is effective because it targets the root causes of frozen shoulder: inflammation, pain, and stiffness. By combining these three tinctures, you can address all aspects of the condition, providing relief and promoting recovery.
supplementary_avoid: If you have frozen shoulder, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures made from herbs like 'Valeriana officinalis' and 'Paeonia lactiflora'. Valerian root can make you feel drowsy and relaxed, which might worsen frozen shoulder symptoms, especially pain and stiffness. This is because Valeriana officinalis can slow down nerve function and affect muscle movement, potentially making it harder to move your arm. Paeonia lactiflora, or peony root, can also cause drowsiness and interact with some medications, including painkillers often used to treat frozen shoulder. Another tincture to avoid is 'Hydrastis canadensis', or goldenseal, due to its potential to cause bleeding when taken in large quantities, which could be a concern if you're taking blood-thinning medications for frozen shoulder treatment. 'Aloe barbadensis' tincture should also be avoided, as it can cause stomach upset and interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications. Lastly, 'Urtica dioica', or stinging nettle, tincture should be used with caution, as it can cause allergic reactions and interact with medications like blood thinners.
supplementary_other_remedies: Applying heat can help relax the muscles around the shoulder joint, reducing stiffness and pain. A warm bath or shower can do this, but some people prefer to use a heating pad or a warm compress wrapped in a towel to avoid direct heat on the skin. Heat therapy can increase blood flow to the area, bringing in oxygen and nutrients to the damaged tissues, which can aid in the healing process.  Cold therapy, on the other hand, can help reduce inflammation and numb the pain. Ice packs or cold compresses can be applied to the affected area for a short time, but be careful not to leave it on too long, as this can cause more harm than good. Cold therapy can also help reduce swelling, which can put pressure on the nerves and cause pain.  Massage can help loosen up tight muscles and improve range of motion. A physical therapist or a massage therapist can use various techniques to target the affected areas, breaking up scar tissue and promoting blood flow. This can be especially helpful in the early stages of frozen shoulder, when the joint is most stiff.  Rest and relaxation are also essential in managing frozen shoulder. Getting enough sleep and practicing stress-reducing techniques, such as deep breathing or meditation, can help reduce muscle tension and promote healing. This is because stress can cause the body to produce more cortisol, a hormone that can exacerbate inflammation and pain.  Epsom salt baths can also provide relief from frozen shoulder symptoms. The magnesium in Epsom salt can help relax the muscles and reduce inflammation, promoting healing and relaxation. When added to warm water, Epsom salt can be easily absorbed through the skin, providing a natural way to reduce pain and stiffness.  It's also worth noting that some people have found relief from frozen shoulder symptoms by using a device called a TENS unit. This device sends electrical impulses to the affected area, which can help block pain signals to the brain and reduce inflammation. However, this should only be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as it can be uncomfortable or even painful for some people.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with frozen shoulder often experience other painful conditions. Many have tendonitis, which is inflammation of the tendons. Turmeric, a medicinal herb, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling. It works by blocking the production of a chemical called prostaglandin, which causes pain and inflammation.  Some people with frozen shoulder also have osteoarthritis, a condition where the joints wear down over time. Willow bark, another medicinal herb, contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. Salicin helps reduce pain and inflammation by blocking the production of prostaglandin. This can help ease the pain and stiffness associated with osteoarthritis.  Frozen shoulder sufferers may also have bursitis, an inflammation of the fluid-filled sacs that cushion the joints. Ginger, a medicinal herb, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the bursae. Ginger also helps relax the muscles, which can become tense and painful in people with frozen shoulder.  Additionally, people with frozen shoulder often have adhesions, or scar tissue, in the affected shoulder. Arnica, a medicinal herb, can help break down these adhesions and promote healing. Arnica's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce pain and swelling.  Lastly, frozen shoulder sufferers may experience poor circulation, which can make the condition worse. Ginkgo biloba, a medicinal herb, can help improve circulation by dilating the blood vessels. This can help bring more oxygen and nutrients to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing pain.
